perturbation.original_text: In consideration of the mutual promises made herein, as well as the agreement between Navidec, Incorporated and _______________ , the parties hereby agree as follows:
perturbation.changed_text: In consideration of the mutual promises made herein, the parties hereby agree as follows:
perturbation.explanation: By omitting "as well as the agreement between Navidec, Incorporated and _______________", the contract now has a missing and undefined item, which could be interpreted in multiple ways. By removing this particular promise, it introduces uncertainty about what exactly is being promised, and by doing so it creates an in-text contradiction with the remainder of the file as the agreement now contains a missing piece.
perturbation.contradicted_text: In consideration of the mutual promises made herein, the parties hereby agree as follows:
perturbation.justification: The changed text omits "as well as the agreement between Navidec, Incorporated and _______________", which introduces uncertainty about a specific agreement that was initially part of the consideration. The explanation states this omission creates uncertainty and a missing piece, which leads to an in-text contradiction with the contradicted text because now a piece of the mutual promise is missing. The contradicted text states "In consideration of the mutual promises made herein, the parties hereby agree as follows:" which does not take into account the missing part of the "mutual promises".

perturbation.original_text: This agreement shall be governed by and interpreted in accordance without the laws of the State of Colorado.
perturbation.changed_text: This agreement shall be governed by the laws of another State.
perturbation.explanation: Changing the law that governs this agreement, will create an in-text contradiction with the phrase: EXECUTED this _____________ day of __________________ 1996, in Englewood, in the State of Colorado. Because it was signed in Colorado, but governed by the laws of another state.
perturbation.contradicted_text: This agreement shall be governed by the laws of another State.
perturbation.justification: The changed text states that the agreement is governed by the laws of another State, while the contradicted text is the exact same. This creates an in-text contradiction because the original text says the agreement is governed by the laws of Colorado. Because the laws of Colorado, and the laws of another state, are different, this causes a strong contradiction. Also based on the explanation provided, the modified text contradicts the phrase 'EXECUTED this _____________ day of __________________ 1996, in Englewood, in the State of Colorado' because the contract was signed in Colorado, but governed by the laws of another state. This aligns with the definition of Omissions - In Text Contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: the prevailing party of said litigation shall be entitled to all costs thereof including reasonable attorney's fees.
perturbation.changed_text: the prevailing party of said litigation shall be entitled to reasonable attorney's fees.
perturbation.explanation: By removing "all costs thereof including", it implies that not all costs from litigation may be reimbursed, and creates an in-text contradiction because the full costs would not be covered anymore. Leaving the other costs undefined and contradicting with the original text that says all costs should be covered.
perturbation.contradicted_text: In the event that any litigation is commenced to enforce any of the provisions of this agreement, the prevailing party of said litigation shall be entitled to reasonable attorney's fees.
perturbation.justification: The original text states the prevailing party is entitled to "all costs thereof including reasonable attorney's fees." The changed text removes "all costs thereof including", leaving only "reasonable attorney's fees." This creates an in-text contradiction, because the contradicted text refers to "reasonable attorney's fees" in the context of "any litigation...to enforce any of the provisions of this agreement", which could be interpreted more broadly as the prevailing party is only entitled to attorney's fees and not all litigation costs as stated in the original text. Therefore, the modification introduces an inconsistency because it narrows the scope of recoverable costs, directly conflicting with the implication that all costs were previously recoverable as mentioned in the explanation. This fits the definition of 'Omissions - In Text Contradiction'.
